title: In Your Head
editorialSummary: Types of human memory grouped by the psychological category they belong to. Working memory: the temporary scratch-pad — digit span, mental arithmetic, remembering a phone number, keeping track of a sentence. Episodic memory: autobiographical, time-stamped events — your first day of school, a birthday party, last night's dinner, a holiday trip. Semantic memory: general world knowledge facts with no personal timestamp — knowing Paris is in France, knowing what a dog is, capital cities, word meanings. Procedural memory: knowing how to do something physically — riding a bike, typing, swimming, tying a shoelace. Items are descriptive examples, all unambiguous.
